frameworks.name: recurrent neural network response model
frameworks.one_line: RNN with internal memory nonlinearly maps feature sequences to fMRI responses

frameworks.name: encoding model
frameworks.one_line: feature-to-response stage upgraded from linear convolution to RNN

frameworks.name: linear convolution response model
frameworks.one_line: standard linear hemodynamic convolution baseline for response prediction

core_question: Can recurrent neural networks serve as response models that better predict fMRI activity from feature sequences than linear convolution?
key_assumptions: stimuli can be mapped to features via a feature model
feature-to-response mapping benefits from nonlinear temporal processing
hemodynamic responses exhibit long-term temporal dependencies
manipulations: response model class: RNN vs established linear convolution baseline
use of internal recurrent memory over feature sequences
key_findings: RNN response models significantly outperform established response models
RNNs accurately estimate long-term dependencies underlying hemodynamic responses
interpretations: recurrent memory enables better modeling of feature-evoked fMRI response sequences
response-model development is an underexplored axis of encoding-model improvement
